tokens: Well, the next thing it says is that Gene gets out.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: That's what it says on my (cuff) checklist.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: That means you got to get out of the way so I can open the hatch.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Well, I'm going to have to turn around a little, I think, so I can help you.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I tell you at 4-1/2 (psi), you're really pretty heavy (means "stiff").
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: What was that that came shooting up here?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I still got a Water Flag.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Long Pause) How does the water pressure look, Houston?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Challenger, they're looking just a little bit low.
We're still expecting them to build up.
It's going to take a little while.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: I'm getting down on my knees out here.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I'm holding you away from the DEDA...(correcting himself) the DSKY.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I'm going to put this visor down now, I think.
(Pause) How does that look to you?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I think you're getting out though, because there's not as much of you in here as there used to be.
Oh, hey, Gene, when I get down there, I got to fix your tool harness (on the PLSS).
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: It's come off the bottom again.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Well, I can't do it now, because it's come off from the bottom.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Oh, the bottom of the PLSS, huh?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Can you squat down any further, because you're hooked on...(Pause) You're making it worse.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Now, be careful because you might hook it on something down there.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: It's loose on your back; on the back of the PLSS.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Oh, man, I don't like that.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Well, I'll fix it when I get out there.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Houston, the Commander is on the porch of Challenger.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: We copy you, Commander, and your feedwater pressure is looking much better...
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: How am I...(Stops to listen to Bob)
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Everything else look good to you?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I'm going to get the MESA (Modular Equipment Stowage Assembly).
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: And I'll have an ETB ready for you (as per
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (Reaching for the D-ring) Oh, man; oh, man; oh, man.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: All the way down; it looks like.
I jettisoned...Oh, you want an ETB?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: And, the (suit) pressure looks like it's started to stabilize at 3.8.
I don't know whether I'm getting cooler or not, but I feel pretty good.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: How about a jett(ison) bag, too?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (Using the lanyard to lower the ETB to the ground) Oh, Jack, I could swing it over the - it won't be any problem - over the strut.
Okay; and the jett bag is swinging free.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: This looks like a Santa Claus bag.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: The Rover looks in good shape.
I've got all my visors down.
Jack, I wouldn't lower your gold visor until after you get on the porch, because it's plenty dark out here.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Going down the items on cuff checklist page
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: The Commander is about three quarters of the way down.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: And, Houston, as I step off at the surface at Taurus-Littrow, we'd like to dedicate the first step of Apollo 17 to all those who made it possible.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Unbelievable; but is it bright in the Sun.
We landed in a very shallow depression.
That's why we've got a slight pitch-up angle.
(It's a) very shallow, dinner-plate-like, dish crater just about the width of the struts (meaning the total span of the landing gear).
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Getting the circuit breakers verified (as per
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (Pause) Oh, do we have boulder tracks coming down (the side of North Massif).
Let me see exactly where we are.
I think I may be just in front of Punk.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: We copy that, Gene; and are the boulder tracks...
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: On the North Massif we've got very obvious boulder tracks.
A couple of large boulders come within 20 or 30 feet of the (break in slope)...Looks like where we can get to them; there's a couple I know we can get to.
Well, the Sun angle is such that, what I saw on the South Massif earlier, I can't see very well.
But, I know there were boulder tracks over there.
Bear Mountain...Boy, it's hard to look to the east (toward the Sculptured Hills).
Bear Mountain and the Sculptured Hills have a very, very similar texture on the surface.
The Sculptured Hills' (texture) is like the wrinkled skin of an old, old, 100-year-old man.
(That) is probably the best way I could put it.
Very, very hummocky, but smoothly pockmarked.
I do not see any boulders up on the Sculptured Hills from here.
But it's awful hard to look to the east and to the southeast.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Have you got an LMP with you yet?
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Jack, let me make sure...We didn't have an awful lot of dust on landing; but I can dig my foot in 8 or 10 inches, and I know we're at least that thick.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: There's a small little 1-meter crater right in front of us with a whole mess of glass right in the middle.
That's right in front of the MESA, as a matter of fact.
Right where I want to park the Rover.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Gene is walking around the LM to get his bearings) I'm going to take a quick look back.
I think this is Poppie, and I can give you a real better idea where we are.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I'm not going to lock it.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: We've got to go back there.
You lose the key, and we're in trouble.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Who said this place was smooth?
There's a lot of local depressions here I didn't figure existed.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Hey, who's been tracking up my lunar surface?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Hey, Bob, I'm east of the LM now.

I'm east of the LM, and the back strut of the LM is...Well, the LM straddles this crater I talked about, and that's where we get the pitch angle; the back strut is probably right down in the eastern one-third of that crater.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Hey, man; you had some forward velocity.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: That's what I wanted to have.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Boy, I look at some of these rocks that are filleted here, Jack, and there sure are a lot of sparklies in them.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I'm going to get to work in a minute, just as soon as I take a look at Trident (actually Poppie).
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Why don't you come over here and let me deploy your
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Just walk around for one second.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Laughs) Hey, man, put your visor down.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: And, I'll be over there, and you can fix my tool harness.
I don't like that thing loose.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I don't like it loose, either.
What are you doing over there (by Poppie)?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I was just going to give them a fix (meaning another estimate of where they landed).
All these little craters, Jack, have got glass in the bottom of them.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (To Houston) There's very clear sweeping of the surface by the descent plume out, oh, about 10 meters.
(Pause) (To Gene) Come over here, and I'll fix your antenna.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Hey, Bob, how big is Poppie supposed to be?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: It looks on the map like it's about 75 meters in diameter.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Okay, I tell you where I think I landed...Oh, about 100 meters from Poppie at 10 o'clock.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Letting himself be talked out of it) Well, I know.
I got to look around a little more.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: It might be part of Trident.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I don't want you to stand up yet.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: The surface is moderately cohesive, which holds a pretty good bootprint.
Gene's (garbled) looks very much like previous soils (that is, soils at other Apollo landing sites).
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: You got a hole behind you now.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Well, I'll stand in it, and you can get at it better.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Well, you got me right in (that is, facing) the Sun.
Can you come around this way?
(Laughs) I'm going to have to get upstream of you.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Look, you get up on the hill, and I'll get in the hole.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Boy, your feet look like you just...
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Finishing the thought) Walked on the Moon, huh?
Well, I tell you Gene, I think the next generation ought to accept this as a challenge.
(Pause) Let's see them leave footsteps like these someday.
Got another...Well, that'll be all right.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: What did you do with my tool harness?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I'm going to work on it; that's what I'm going to do.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (Pause) Boy, I tell you, looking to the east, you might just as well forget it.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: How's this thing...I'm going to have to loosen it.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Well, if you could just stretch it around.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: But I will be in a minute.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Don't loosen it to the point where you can't get it back on.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Pause) Somebody tied you on wrong, too.
They've got the strap reversed for the Velcro.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: If it doesn't, I'll fix you again.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Man, there's sparklies in the soil, Jack.
You can just look at it.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Yeah, I think that's little (pieces of) glass.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Let's go back here and get to work, and I'll show you that crater that's got nothing but glass in the bottom.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: That's a vesicular rock of some kind there, Geno.
It almost looks like a Mono Craters (California) pumice, but don't quote me.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Even the small, even the very small - the 1- and 2-inch, 3-inch - fragments that are laying around here - have been dusted and filleted...
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: And that sweeping by the descent stage goes all the way out there, Houston, to where we were; which was about 50 meters, I guess.
Hey, man...(Jack stumbles) Whuh, whuh, whuh, whuh.
Hey, these rocks, they almost have a very light pinkish hue to them, and they're not obviously breccia.
Now, that's like a breccia there.
But this is something else again.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I don't think there is any place you could land around here where you wouldn't have one foot(pad) in a crater.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Looks like a vesicular, very-light-colored porphyry of some kind; it's about 10 or 15 percent vesicles.
I'm right in front (that is, west) of the LM.
Quite a few of the rocks look of that type.
Sort of a pinkish hue to them.
The texture is coarse, but I'm not sure how crystalline they are, yet.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Jack, when you put up the ETB (on the MESA), check down there below it.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay, let me take a look at the Rover.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: That's my fault; I guess (for not packing the ETB carefully enough).
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Oh man, I tell you, we came down at just a little forward velocity.
About a (one)-foot slip on the pad.
I tell you, there's craters all over here.
I'd sure like to think that that (Rover) wheel is where it's supposed to be.
(Pause) Our next little vehicle to work.
(Long Pause while Gene examines the Rover, using cuff checklist page
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (To Parker) Hey, let me ask you.
When I was behind the LM, I could look right into an area and see the bell of the ascent stage.
I never realized that before, but I guess that's normal, huh?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Yeah; We saw it on the (launch) pad (at the Cape).
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Remember when we went out there?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: The only reason I asked, Bob, I'm sure it's normal, and it doesn't look anything's missing, it's just right into the Sun.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Yeah, the consensus of opinion down here is that you can, also.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Well, that's probably the best place in the world to get a consensus of opinion from.
Okay, Jack, it's about work time.
I've got this Rover about ready for your pull up there.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I got a little delayed here.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (Pause) I'm sure glad those guys made us train so hard.
transcript.speaker: Cernan
